text.content:
The Five Truths, and How to Guarantee Them

Tick these as they land. Each is carried by several clues, so a missed roll never costs the party a truth — but if you reach Scene 7 and one is thin, use the backstop line.

The party must believe…Carried byIf it's thin at the gate, use…
1. Haskur was kept alive on purpose.Caelith's speech (two prior “deaths”); the burned focus; the pre-dated letter of credit.Caelith: “Twice before, someone caught him before the ground did. That is not luck. That is a budget.”
2. He's a node in a network, not a lone man.The three-hand ledger fragment; Gilst's four-year retainer; the inside-job forgery.Caelith: “He has had four names that I can prove and a face for none of them. Men like that are issued, not born.”
3. Cindren & Vhal are central; the hand is in Hardby.The seal + countermark; Gilst's Hardby bank instrument; Vesh's “Hardby work”; Tamsin's Hardby queries; the chase fold.Caelith, tapping the bank instrument: “One chop. On his money and on the dead man's. Hardby drew them both.”
4. Loftwick won't let them finish here.Moll's bribe; Sarth's night; the Pressure Clock's escalations; the watcher.Let the next clock segment fire — its telegraph is the proof.
5. The road runs east, and now.The six converging threads; Tamsin “delayed,” not “settled”; Caelith's conclusion.Trina: “The thing you are looking for is not in Loftwick. I will push where Caelith will not.”

The line the phase must land

Haskur Vandrell did not flee to safety. He fled to an account someone had already opened for him.

Notes on each truth

  • Truth 1 (by design) is the spine of Caelith's morning speech. Onnwal 575; Hardby 580; Loftwick now. The two prior “deaths” are the proof. If the speech got short-shrifted, the burned focus carries it (Arcana DC 13: a contingency-class spell was channeled through it). If even that didn't land, the pre-dated letter of credit drawn six weeks before the fight is the third leg.
  • Truth 2 (network) falls out of the ledger fragment by itself. Three names. Three different clerks' hands. No sums. A ledger without sums is a roster, and a roster turns Haskur into an account, not an agent.
  • Truth 3 (Hardby) is over-determined. The Hardby chop on Haskur's escrow + the same chop on Gilst's hidden bank instrument is the proof of one hand; Vesh's “reads like Hardby work”; Tamsin's last three queries about Hardby chops; Veth's missing original carbons; the chase fold's east on the Sevenday cart. Six threads, one city. The party will draw the line themselves.
  • Truth 4 (Loftwick won't let them finish) is delivered by the Pressure Clock and its consequences. If the party hasn't felt it by Scene 6, run the next clock segment as written.
  • Truth 5 (the road, now) is the verb delayed. Hold Caelith to it: “I do not say it as a comfort. I say it because the verb is not 'settled.' She is somewhere they have not yet decided to close her account.” Alive, and on a clock.

The Pressure Clock in Full

Put six segments on a notecard where the players can see them. Advance it when the conspiracy can see the party act (showing their hand at the firm; rattling Pollow; a public spectacle; the Sarth night) and when a whole day passes with nothing done. Don't advance it for quiet, honest work. Announce the trigger each time, and read the telegraph aloud — every tick is a gift of momentum, not a punishment.

The six segments

Seg.What the party sees that morningWhat it means
1 — TamperingCaelith: an item in the chest sits one inch from where he set it. “Someone was looking. Not for what we expected.”The cellar isn't safe; the firm has a hand inside.
2 — WarningTrina lays a note on the table: someone has told her to stop hosting the party. “The neighbor is mistaken.” She doesn't budge.The firm knows who their hosts are. It's personal now.
3 — RumorA broadsheet at midday asks what the Audit Hall keeps in its little palace, and who is foreign in the question. By tea-time the party is recognizable in the street.Visibility now has a price (and summons Moll).
4 — Witness lossCut the thread that hurts most: Pollow found dead, or Anwic reassigned, or Gilst's rooms cleaned out before the party returns.Dawdling has a cost. Urgency, purchased the hard way.
5 — The motionElsbet hands Caelith a Council card. He reads it standing. “The motion is filed.” The Audit Hall withdraws its quiet support; the party has 48 hours.A fuse. It pushes them to the gate.
6 — The door shutsGM's choice, used only if the clock truly burns out: Tamsin confirmed dead, or Caelith recalled, or Vellin taken.The next chapter begins under worse conditions.

Advances by player action

ActionClock advance
Visit C&V Wick Lane & show endorsement+1
Confront Pollow+1
Public Watch escalation on Moraven+1
Decline or defer Moll's offer+1 (also triggers Sarth)
Each session day without progress+1
Sarth retrieval resolves (any outcome)+1

If it runs hot

If you reach segment 5 with threads still open, the conspiracy is closing in too fast. Cool the burn by spending in-fiction capital:

  • Have Caelith burn a favor. He absorbs a Council move at a personal political cost and the segment-5 effects are delayed by 24 hours. He says, “I have paid for one more morning. Use it.”
  • Move Sarth earlier. Resolving the Sarth scene gives the party a tangible win and a captured contractor; it also ticks the clock, but the win is morale-positive and re-energises tired tables.
  • Cut to departure. The Three Roads East scene is itself the pressure release. If the party is at clock 5 and pushing back, simply ask them: “Caelith would like to meet you in the Old Council Chamber at first light. Are you there?”

If it runs cold

If the party is at clock 1 going into the last session, the conspiracy is too quiet. Knock louder:

  • Trigger Moll early. A second visit, this time with a clerk and a quiet threat: “The motion is being drafted as we speak.”
  • Have Trina recommend departure. She says, in her quiet voice, “The thing you are looking for is not in Loftwick. It is east of here. Caelith will not push. I will push.”
  • Have Caelith pin the map. He places the small flat brass token on Rel Astra on the map and says, “If we keep working at Loftwick speed, we will lose Tamsin and we will lose Ailen. I would prefer not to.”

What never to do

  • Do not roll behind the screen for a clock advance. Clocks are deterministic. They tick because a trigger fired.
  • Do not punish curiosity with the clock. Re-reading the evidence in the Refectory does not advance the clock. Visiting the C&V satellite does. The difference is whether the conspiracy can detect the action from outside the building.
  • Do not skip telegraphs. Every milestone has a small in-fiction signal. Read the signal.
  • Do not let the clock fire on a session-break. If you're going to advance from 2 to 3, do it during the session so the table sees it land. End the session with the clock state visible.

If They Wander — Getting Them Back on Course

The party can't break this chapter, only slow it. When they drift, reach for the gentlest tool that works and climb only as needed.

The ladder of nudges

  1. Let a thread tug. Trina mentions, in passing, that Ailen is alone in Hardby. The human stake re-aims a drifting party better than any map.
  2. Let the clock tick for the wasted day and read its telegraph — each segment is a fresh event that hands the party something to do.
  3. Let an ally push. Caelith pins the map; Trina says the thing Caelith won't. They have standing to be blunt; use it.
  4. Collapse a thread they were dawdling on — fire a witness-loss segment. Losing something re-prices everything.

Specific drifts and what to do about them

  • They skip Gilst's rooms. The Hardby chop still reaches them through Vesh's “Hardby work” and the chase fold, and Caelith's contact can recover the bank instrument before the room is cleaned.
  • They skip Tannerway. Trina carries Ailen and the Sevenday note to them unasked, and Anwic can bring Tamsin's file to Caelith.
  • They never trigger the chase. The watcher tails them home and tips the firm — folding into Moll and Sarth — and the fold's pointer is redundant with five other threads.
  • They catch the watcher instantly. You lose nothing: the fold is the prize, and now there's a frightened girl and a choice.
  • They take Moll's bribe. Run downtime, then send Caelith with the bill (see Scene 5). Tamsin is dead. Vellin and Ailen are gone. Pollow is dead. Caelith is being recalled. The conspirators used the time the party did not.
  • They try to ride straight past Hardby to Rel Astra. Let them — but price it in Caelith's voice first: no Tamsin, no Tarsh, no Veska Maelan, no Castrian Vell, and a far harder next chapter.
  • They try to storm the firm's seniors now. The seniors are in Rel Astra behind a wall the Loftwick satellite genuinely cannot open — that's real, in-fiction, not your hand on the scale.
  • They insist on splitting across both neighborhoods. Call them to a working lunch at the Little Palace to pool what they found; the half-day costs a clock segment, and the table stays together.

The Roadhouse south of Loftwick ("R." on Haskur's map)

If the party finds the “R.” mark on the third caravan-route map (Investigation DC 14 confirms it matches Haskur's spellbook hand) and asks Caelith about it, the hook leads to the optional sidequest at the Sign of the Brown Hare — a small roadhouse two days' ride south on the Wool Road. See Phase 2 — Sidequest: The Sign of the Brown Hare for the full chapter.

Short summary for the at-the-table card: Haskur has a half-sister, Rosalin Brent, who runs the roadhouse and has watched him become a stranger over thirty years. No conspiracy intelligence is on offer at the Hare; what the party gets instead is the sister's grief, a thesis on her brother (“Hask was never clever. Someone with a head on their shoulders put work in his hands.”) that ratifies what they are starting to suspect, and an optional never-sent letter that pays off in Phase 6 if delivered to Haskur at the Brass Crow.

Cost: 5 in-fiction days off the spine; +2 Pressure Clock segments (one each on the return ride). Decline if the clock is already at 3+; recommend if the table needs the relief beat before Movement Three's squeeze.

Things You Never Name

The chapter changes how the party moves, never what stays secret. Hold these exactly:

  • “E.” is never identified — not a name, not a people, not a faction. The party collects the initial and leaves with the question open. (There is an answer. It is years away from this table.)
  • The lacquered tube is “elven, but peculiar” — never anything more. Under any roll, the peculiarity is the answer. The strangest word the campaign is protecting does not get spoken here, and legend lore yields only a fragment about a people who “walked downward and were forgotten.”
  • HARBOR BEFORE THRONE stays a slogan of intent — no throne, no harbor, no succession named yet.
  • The leak in Caelith's office is implied, never pinned. The inside forgery, the tampering, and Sarth's forged seal all point inward — and Caelith will not name a clerk he can't prove. Neither do you.
  • Trina's patron is never named, not even to Alicia. Past her wall lies only: “I share a teacher. I have not finished my lessons.”
  • The chase fold points at the road, not the mastermind — a route and a firm-mark, never “E.” or the tube's craft.

The four sentences to memorize

Have these on the tip of your tongue. They are the safe answers the party will press you on.

  1. “E. is what is on the page. There is no other answer in this room.”
  2. “The lacquer-laying is elven, but in a tradition no Yeomanry sage can place. The most accurate sentence you can offer is, 'elven, but peculiar.'”
  3. “Power flows through ports before crowns notice. That is older than the Yeomanry. It is also a slogan — which is what people write down when they intend to do something the law will not yet name.” (Caelith on HARBOR BEFORE THRONE.)
  4. “I will not name a clerk to you whom I cannot prove. If I named one, and you treated them as proven, I would have made you the wrong kind of Hand.” (Caelith on the leak in his own office.)

If you slip

Don't panic-retcon. “That's what's on the page. There's no other answer in this room.” The campaign survives one slip.

Treat any confirmation you accidentally offered as a working hypothesis the party has — they still need real evidence to act on it, and the road still runs east. Move on; the chapter does not depend on perfect concealment, only on the fact that the party leaves with the question open, not closed.

Hand-off to the Next Chapter

The road the party chose in Scene 7 sets the conditions the next chapter opens under. Price the choice; don't editorialize it — none of these is a failure.

Road takenTime the firm has hadTamsin Moraven, in HardbyTheir Hardby contacts
Official Delegation (authority)~2 weeksConfirmed dead — a body recovered days before they arrive.Witnesses scattered; the broker moved upcountry; a paper trail only.
Commercial Cover (cover)~1 weekAlive, moved to a coastal estate two days east — recoverable with effort.Veska Maelan and Castrian Vell both reachable; the broker confrontable.
Quiet Pursuit (distance)~3 daysAlive, still in Hardby — held in a warehouse, a live rescue.Every contact unprepared and within reach; the broker easiest of all.

The chase fold pays off here too

“East on the Sevenday cart” is the near end of a courier chain the party meets again in Hardby. When they recognize the mark, let it be a small earned “we've seen this before” — a thread tied off, not a shortcut taken.

What the party leaves with

However Phase 2 resolves, the party rides east with at minimum:

  • The Hardby chop. The matched chop on Haskur's escrow and Gilst's bank instrument is the irrefutable physical proof.
  • The ledger fragment. Three names, three hands, no sums. The roster.
  • The strip of vellum. HARBOR BEFORE THRONE. Memorized, undischarged.
  • The lacquered tube. Elven, but peculiar. The deepest question they carry forward.
  • The seed pouch. Sterile durum + the dry barony. The grain fraud that ties Haskur to Tamsin's work.
  • The brass token. Caelith's quill-above-closed-gate, the seal that lets the party write back to him from any city.
  • Trina's letter to Castrian Vell (if they take Commercial Cover) and her quiet relationship with Alicia (always).

Optional carry-forwards

  • Sarth's testimony, if they captured her cleanly — one name (“Tarsh”, a Hardby intermediary) and the description of laundered drafts from banks that aren't Cindren & Vhal.
  • Pollow's two-day window if they granted it — Caelith's Hardby contacts keep him as a live witness.
  • Sergeant Anwic's notes if he was reassigned before they left, pressed into their hands privately as a parting gift.
  • Sparrow herself if they caught her and chose mercy — Trina takes her in; she will be in Loftwick when they return.

pages.name: The Cast at a Glance + Full NPC Matrix

text.content:
The Cast at a Glance

The shorthand: who's a friend, who's a witness, who's an obstacle, and who never comes on stage.

Allies

  • Caelith Dunivar — the Hand of the Duke; answers facts, refuses guesses, will not name an unproven leak.
  • Trina Alvere — his advisor, Alicia's quiet mentor; warm, observant, immovable; her patron is never named.
  • Elsbet Vael — the steward; knows the household, discreet about everything that matters.

Witnesses

  • Doman Reith — Gilst's frightened landlord.
  • Doril Veth — Tamsin's grieving chief clerk.
  • Vellin Moraven — her husband; the Sevenday note.
  • Sgt Halir Anwic — the watchman who let her file cool, looking to make it right.

Obstacles

  • Hesren Vesh — the firm's stiff, frightened Loftwick clerk; not a conspirator.
  • Reln Pollow — the contract physician who signed Gilst away; spare him to keep the witness.
  • Arthen Moll — the sincere Deputy Voice with a bribe; not corrupt, only certain.
  • Merev Sarth — the retrieval thief; here for the evidence, not for blood; yields rather than dies.
  • “Sparrow” (Anla Coyle) — the posted watcher; a hungry kid who knows the lanes; runs, drops the fold, and gets away.

Never on stage (and never named)

  • The Hardby hand who underwrote Haskur.
  • The broker “Tarsh.”
  • “E.” above the firm.
  • Whoever made the lacquered tube.

Full NPC Knowledge Matrix

One row per Phase 2 NPC. Columns: what they know freely, what they know but will not say, what they do not know, how to pry harder.

Allies (full detail)

Caelith Dunivar — Hand of the Duke

  • Freely: Haskur's nine-year history (Onnwal 575, Hardby 580, Loftwick now). The Hand of the Duke title. The Yeomanry's three coronial physicians, and the fact that Pollow isn't one of them. The Audit Hall procedural map of Loftwick.
  • Withholds: The name of the Duke (a GM-level decision, not a Caelith decision). The name of any specific Audit Hall clerk he suspects (he refuses to name an unproven suspect). The contents of Trina's private hospitality arrangement with him.
  • Does not know: Who “E.” is. What “Harbor Before Throne” refers to specifically. The identity of the Hardby underwriter. The location of Tamsin Moraven. Where the lacquered tube was actually made (he reads the craftsmanship as “elven but peculiar” like everyone else).
  • Prying: He answers factual questions readily. He refuses speculation about people he cannot prove. Insight DC 14 reads his hesitation about the Audit Hall clerk; the party can offer to help him hunt that clerk, which he accepts.

Trina Alvere — Patron of the Loftwick Arts

  • Freely: Her cordial refusal of two Cindren & Vhal “small loans”. Her sponsorship of Tamsin Moraven's daughter Ailen. Her sponsorship of Castrian Vell (the Hardby poet). Her warm welcome and standing offer of safe lodging at her townhouse on Civic Way.
  • Withholds: Her warlock pact (presented to her as “the Quiet Patroness”). The fact that she recognises Alicia's pact-bond on sight. Her stat block.
  • Does not know: Who “E.” is. The full mechanics of the C&V conspiracy — she only knows the firm's odor. Where Tamsin is being held.
  • Prying: She speaks plainly when asked plainly. She will not be cornered. If pressed about her own patron she replies, “I share a teacher with you. I have not finished my lessons.” She means it. Insight DC 18 reads her as hiding more than embarrassment; do not give the patron's name regardless of the roll.

Elsbet Vael — Steward of the Little Palace

  • Freely: The household's daily routine. Who has visited at what time. Where Caelith keeps his post and his keys. The schedules of the militia rotation at the gate.
  • Withholds: Nothing meaningful. She is scrupulously discreet about Caelith's personal affairs without being secretive about the household's operation.
  • Does not know: Anything about the conspiracy. She is staff, not an investigator.
  • Prying: Persuasion DC 12 unlocks the daily routine in detail. She refuses to discuss the contents of the Strong Room and would inform Caelith if asked.

Sources & witnesses

Hesren Vesh — C&V Loftwick satellite agent

  • Freely: The firm's public Charter Roll of Marks. The firm's official services. Polite procedural refusals.
  • Withholds: The fact that he has been ordered to report any party that shows the Vandrell endorsement. The fact that he has already cast a wax of the Strong Room's second lock during a Yeomanry inspection. The fact that he is afraid of his Hardby seniors.
  • Does not know: Who the Hardby underwriter is by name. What is in the lacquered tube. Who “E.” is. The conspiracy at any layer above his branch.
  • Prying: Persuasion DC 18 or Intimidation DC 17 yields, “The endorsement reads like Hardby work, not Loftwick work.” He sends a courier to Hardby the moment the party leaves regardless of how the conversation went. Insight DC 14 reads him as disturbed by the endorsement.

Doman Reith — Gilst's landlord

  • Freely: The fact that a man with a Watch tabard searched Gilst's rooms a day ago. The man's description (vague). His own anxiety about the situation.
  • Withholds: Nothing strategic; he is genuinely a careful old landlord who knows he should have refused.
  • Does not know: Anything about Gilst's hidden material. Anything about the conspiracy.
  • Prying: Insight DC 13 confirms he is not lying. He yields entry willingly with Caelith's written request.

Reln Pollow — Contract physician (Gilst's killer)

  • Freely: That he certified Gilst's death. That it was “straightforward.” A description of Gilst's apparent peaceful end.
  • Withholds: That he was paid to falsify. That he expects to be killed next. The name of the intermediary who paid him (he does not know it — the contract came by note).
  • Does not know: Who “E.” is. The name of his paymaster. The full extent of the conspiracy.
  • Prying: Persuasion DC 18 or Intimidation DC 16 yields his nerves but nothing actionable. The party can offer him a two-day departure deadline in exchange for not pulling his license. If denied: he is poisoned the next evening and the party loses a witness. Caelith strongly recommends granting it.

Sergeant Halir Anwic — Yeomanry Watch

  • Freely: His thin missing-person report on Tamsin Moraven. His own embarrassment about it.
  • Withholds: Nothing.
  • Does not know: Anything about C&V or the conspiracy.
  • Prying: Will reopen the case at Caelith's request. Hands over his notes as a gift if Pressure Clock segment 4 is about to remove him.

Doril Veth — Tamsin's chief clerk

  • Freely: Tamsin's last open ledger book. The clean conclusion of her standing accounts. His own grief.
  • Withholds: Nothing. He has been quietly cataloguing his employer's queries because he expected she would want to resume them.
  • Does not know: Where Tamsin is. The conspiracy.
  • Prying: Investigation DC 12 finds the last three queries reference C&V Hardby chops. DC 15 finds the original carbons of two queries have been removed and replaced with impression-only pages.

Vellin Moraven — Tamsin's husband

  • Freely: Tamsin's note (“If I am not back by Sevenday, take Ailen out of Hardby”). His own quiet grief.
  • Withholds: Nothing.
  • Does not know: Anything about the conspiracy.
  • Prying: Insight DC 12 tells the party he should send for Ailen tonight. Suggesting this gently is the right move. Trina, if present, will write the letter for him.

Antagonists & obstacles

Arthen Moll — Deputy Voice of Civic Reconciliation

  • Freely: His entire pitch (delivered verbatim in the Interruptions journal). His sincere belief that loud strangers damage the Yeomanry's eastern trade. The 1,200 gp honorarium offer.
  • Withholds: Nothing — he is not hiding anything. He is, however, repeating someone else's framing about “the eastern banks watch” (Insight DC 15 catches the borrowed line).
  • Does not know: He is not corrupt. He has not been told anything about the conspiracy. He believes he is mediating a trade dispute.
  • Prying: He is the wall the soft-pressure faction puts in front of inquiries. Insight DC 18 reveals this is the same script he has run three times this season.

Merev Sarth — Retrieval contractor

  • Freely (after capture, after 2 days): She was paid three drafts of 200 gp each (none Cindren & Vhal). The intermediary was a Hardby contact called “Tarsh” — thin man, fifties, smoker's voice.
  • Withholds (always): Her broker. She will die rather than name them.
  • Does not know: Anything about the conspiracy above Tarsh. What was in the Strong Room beyond “retrieve or burn.” Who underwrites Vandrell.
  • Prying: Caelith's standing offer (a Yeomanry passport plus 100 gp for one future testimony) plus Persuasion DC 14 gets her cooperation. She is a contractor, not a fanatic.

Anla “Sparrow” Coyle — the posted watcher

  • Freely (if caught): That a man she never got a clear look at left coppers and a chalk-stub at a dead-letter niche behind the loose ashlar at the Coopers' Arms, on the Tannerway corner. The job: watch the stationer's door, mark who goes up to the dead clerk's rooms and who calls on the green-coats, leave a tally every Sevenday, keep nothing.
  • Withholds: Nothing significant — she is a frightened sixteen-year-old who barely knows anything.
  • Does not know: Who pays her. What it's for. Anything about the conspiracy.
  • Prying: Free if caught; the prize is the fold, not the person.

Off-screen / unmet in Phase 2

NameWhere they areWhat they know
Veshen Cindren (Rel Astra)C&V senior partner, public-facingThe firm's general operations; not the deep client. Phase 4.
Tarlith Vhal Sereth (Rel Astra)C&V senior partner, reclusiveThe deep client. The full conspiracy at his layer. Phase 4 boss.
Tarsh (Hardby intermediary)Hardby underworldHow to hire Sarth. The Hardby underwriter's name. Phase 3.
The Hardby underwriterC&V Hardby branch, seniorThe chop. The Vandrell escrow. “E.”'s instructions. Phase 3.
Veska Maelan (Hardby weighmaster)Hardby trade-clearing notaryTamsin's correspondence partner. Phase 3 key witness.
Castrian Vell (Hardby poet)Hardby salonsTrina's man. Knows everyone. Phase 3 social entry.
The Audit Hall clerk (unnamed)Loftwick, Caelith's own officeThe Strong Room contents at any moment. Resolved later.

Maps & Locations

Every map in Phase 2, with its Foundry Scene name (in the Phase 2 Scenes compendium), which beat it serves, and what's defined on it.

The Little Palace (three floors)

Caelith's residence in Loftwick. The party stays here for the duration of Phase 2. The Phase 1 Scenes pack contains all three floors as drag-ready Foundry scenes with full walls, doors, windows, and lighting.

Main floor

  • Scene: Little Palace — Main Floor (Phase 1 Scenes)
  • Used in: Morning Evidence Review (M4 Refectory), Records Alcove (M5 Library, with hidden alcove M5a behind a wall panel), Moll's visit (M5 Library, moved from M2 Reception Parlor), Sarth's entry (M8 South Service Gate → M12 Spiral Stair).
  • Defined: all perimeter walls; all interior walls dividing rooms; double doors at the front entry, single doors throughout interior; window-walls (sight-blocking, sound-permeable) at the courtyard-facing glazed panels; lamp lights at every visible niche; hearth lights at every visible fireplace; chandelier lights in the Refectory; daylight at the leaded windows by day, lamp-only by night.
  • Room codes: M0 Courtyard Garden · M1 Foyer · M2 Reception Parlor · M3 Sitting Room · M4 Refectory · M5 Library / Records Room · M5a Records Alcove (hidden) · M6 Kitchen · M7 Pantry / Buttery · M8 South Service Gate · M9 Servants' Hall · M10 Footman's Duty Room · M11 Private Chapel · M12 Spiral Stair Vestibule.

Second floor

  • Scene: Little Palace — Second Floor (Phase 1 Scenes)
  • Used in: Three Approaches scene (U4 Old Council Chamber); party bedrooms throughout (U5–U10); Caelith's adjoining suite (U2/U2a) where the Strong-Room alarm rune's chime carries.
  • Defined: Upper Gallery (U1) wrapping three sides of the open courtyard with a balcony rail toward the courtyard; six PC bedrooms; Caelith's suite + study; Trina's guest suite; Old Council Chamber with hearth; bath / water closet; linen closet; lamp lights at every gallery lamp; hearth lights in U2 and U4; daylight from gallery skylights.
  • Room codes: U1 Upper Gallery · U2 Caelith's Civic Office · U2a Caelith's Bedchamber + Sitting · U3 Trina's Guest Suite · U4 Old Council Chamber · U5–U10 PC Bedrooms (six) · U11 Shared Bath / Water Closet · U12 Linen / Cedar Closet.

Cellar

  • Scene: Little Palace — Cellar (Phase 1 Scenes)
  • Used in: Strong Room (C5); Sarth's retrieval-night route ends here.
  • Defined: all perimeter walls; vault interior wall for the Strong Room with a single iron door (double-locked, Sleight of Hand DC 18 ×2); secret door on the Old Service Tunnel (C7) east end; lamp lights at the cellar entry (C1) and at the cistern (C6); no daylight at this level.
  • Room codes: C1 Cellar Entry · C2 Wine Cellar · C3 Pantry Cellar · C4 Coal & Lamp-oil Stores · C5 Strong Room · C6 Old Cistern · C7 Old Service Tunnel.

Phase 2 location scenes

Cindren & Vhal Loftwick Satellite (Wick Lane)

  • Scene: Records Alcove (Cindren & Vhal Loftwick) (Phase 2 Scenes) — note the scene's name is historical; the map is the satellite office, not the records alcove inside the Library.
  • Used in: Thread A — the firm visit. Two storeys visible: a public hall with a railing-divided front and back, and an upper level with three private interview rooms and Hesren Vesh's office.
  • Defined: perimeter walls; the railing divider as a sight-permeable barrier (not a wall); doors on all four interview rooms; lamp lights at each desk lamp; daylight at the small leaded windows.

Reln Pollow's Office

  • Scene: Doctor Pollow's Office (Phase 2 Scenes).
  • Used in: Thread B — Pollow confrontation. Two storeys visible: clerk antechamber below (with strongbox at the desk's right hand); inner office above with the examination desk and Pollow's reference shelves.
  • Defined: perimeter walls; door between antechamber and inner office; lamp lights on both desks; hearth in inner office.

Vellin Moraven's Home (Tannerway)

  • Scene: Vellin Moraven's Home (Phase 2 Scenes).
  • Used in: Thread C — Vellin's heartbreak scene; potentially the Ailen-return scene in Phase 3 if Trina's letter went out.
  • Defined: two floors. Ground floor: kitchen-dining (front, hearth-lit), small study (Vellin's), entry. Upper floor: bedroom + bedroom + bath. All walls; all doors; lamp + hearth lights; daylight at all front-facing windows.

Tamsin Moraven's Counting House (Tannerway)

  • Scene: Tamsin's Counting House (Phase 2 Scenes).
  • Used in: Thread C — Doril Veth shows the party Tamsin's last ledger.
  • Defined: single-floor counting room with a standing desk at the front, weighbench at the rear, bound carbons in a wall niche, Tamsin's private chair behind the standing desk (now empty).

Merro Gilst's Rented Rooms (above the stationer's, Wick Lane)

  • Scene: Gilst's Rented Rooms (Phase 2 Scenes).
  • Used in: Thread B — the optional investigation. Two-room flat: sitting parlor / study, small bedchamber, water-closet under the stair.
  • Defined: all walls, door to the stair-landing, doors between rooms; the seven hidden-item locations marked as notes on the scene.

Trina Alvere's Townhouse (Civic Way)

  • Scene: Trina's Townhouse (Phase 2 Scenes).
  • Used in: Quiet offsite meetings when the Little Palace feels watched; the originals of the Strong Room evidence are stored here on Sarth's retrieval night.
  • Defined: two floors. Ground floor: receiving parlor + small library + kitchen + private back garden with a service door onto a side lane. Upper: Trina's chambers + guest room (Alicia uses this on quiet nights).

The Sparrow's Run (the chase)

The chase scene uses the Loftwick — City scene with token vision on. Lay a light chain of street walls along Wick Lane → the Cloth Market → the Loft Steps → the Tanners' cut → the culvert, and mark the four stretches and the fold-drop point at the Loft Steps. The map's own notes flag it for exactly this.

Notes on Foundry usage

Every Phase 2 scene ships with:

  • Walls for every interior wall and every perimeter wall.
  • Doors at every interior door (Foundry door type 1 = standard hinge; type 2 = secret), including the records-alcove panel in M5 (secret door) and the Old Service Tunnel grate in C7 (secret door).
  • Windows as sight-permeable / sound-permeable wall segments (Foundry: sight: 0, sound: 10 or similar).
  • Lights at every visible lamp niche, hearth, and chandelier in the underlying art, plus daylight settings where appropriate.
  • Notes for key evidence drop points where applicable (the seven items in Gilst's rooms, the Strong Room chest in C5, the records-alcove panel in M5).

The Foundry Scene grid is 5 ft per 100 px on the palace maps. Phase 2 location scenes use a 5 ft grid sized to their underlying images.
